title: ranking.signal v1
description: Retrieval-behaviour signal consumed by the ranking service (V-T5) to feed its online feature store (D17 two-phase ranking). Each event is an impression / click / order observation for a merchant that the ranking model turns into popularity + conversion (CTR) features used to re-rank the search top-500 -> top-50. Keyed by aggregate:merchant_id; at 100M scale merchant fan-out topics use SALTED keys `merchant_id#0..15` (D11) with PER-SALT ordering only. Signals are running-aggregate INCREMENTS (order does not matter across salts), so the consumer needs only EXACTLY-ONCE effect (libs/inbox) — a redelivered signal must never double-count. D30: additive-only within this topic. See contracts/events/README-per-salt-ordering.md.

id.description: mer_ token — the partition key (merchant_id); salted to merchant_id#0..15 for fan-out (D11).

merchant_id.description: mer_ token the signal is about.

signal_type.description: observed behaviour: impression (shown in a feed), click (opened), order (completed purchase).

weight.description: signal magnitude (default 1); e.g. an order's item count or a batched impression count.
